column1: # Supporting equations eq:12.7–12.9 and the Double-Debt Continuity Chain narrative.
#   Total debt payments: ~112 million gold francs paid (NYT "The Ransom"
#   Original indemnity: 150M francs (1825); reduced to 90M francs by agreement.
#   Double-debt mechanism: Haiti borrowed from French banks (principally CIC) at
#   high interest to service the indemnity → original debt + interest on loans = "double debt."
#   Creditor transitions: CIC (1825–1880) → CIC + Banque Nationale d'Haïti (1880–early 1900s) →
#   National City Bank (Citibank precursor) acquires stake (early 1900s) → Citibank dominant
#   Annual payment series: approximated using known endpoints and creditor transitions.

column2: creditor=CIC): 90M franc principal + predatory loan interest.
creditor=CIC/BNH): Central Bank under CIC ownership.

column1: #       Approximate annual payment: 0.8M francs/year (BNH extracts double via central bank control).
#       Approximate annual payment: 0.9M francs/year (Citibank imposes new predatory loans).

column2: if retained in Haiti and invested at 2% real annual return
compounded from payment year to 2022. NYT low estimate: $21B USD 2022.

column1: #     NYT high estimate: $115B USD (using higher counterfactual growth rate).
#     Here we use the $21B low-end: starting from total payments ~$570M 2022-USD equivalent
#     compound value assumes avg payment year ~1886
#     (consistent with historical US equity returns as counterfactual benchmark per NYT).
#   All USD values are 2022 constant dollars for comparability with NYT investigation.

column8: notes
Initial indemnity 150M francs reduced to 90M; first payment under naval duress
CIC predatory loan taken to make first payment; double-debt begins
Haiti earthquake 1842 disrupts economy further
Dominican Republic achieves independence from Haiti
Haiti finally recognizes DR independence after 4 more invasions
Haiti recognizes DR independence (42 years after DR declared it)
Payment disrupted by internal instability
CIC creates Banque Nationale d'Haiti (BNH) — owned by CIC not Haitian govt
BNH central bank extracts additional value via monetary control
Firmin publishes De l'Égalité (same year) — suppressed
National City Bank (Citibank precursor) acquires stake in BNH
NCB increasing control; Haiti transfers gold reserves to NYC
World War I begins; Haiti debt payments continue
US invades Haiti; NCB lobbied US government for intervention
US also invades DR this year; joint Hispaniola occupation begins
US rewrites Haitian constitution to allow foreign land ownership
Corvée (forced labor) reintroduced by US occupation forces
Citibank buys out CIC and European shareholders from BNH
Original 90M franc principal retired; double-debt interest continues
Citibank imposes new predatory loans on Haitian government
US withdraws from DR; continues Haiti occupation
Firestone concession in Liberia same year (parallel extraction pattern)
Wall Street Crash — Haiti still paying Citibank
US withdraws from Haiti (19-year occupation ends)
BNH regains independence from Citibank
Trujillo Parsley Massacre ≈20000 Haitians killed at DR border
World War II; Haiti still making payments
Accelerated payoff begins
World War II ends
FINAL PAYMENT. Double-debt retired. 122 years after 1825 indemnity imposed.
